PLAINTIFF IS FURTHER ORDERED TO PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 1-31. THE COURT ORDERS COUNSEL, MARK HOOSHMAND, TO PAY SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANT OF $2500.00. PAYMENT, RESPONSES AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ARE DUE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: ELIZABETH RILES; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST AND FREDRICK MORSE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, VERIFICATIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS CONTINUED FROM NOV-01-2013 TO DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST AND FREDRICK MORSE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, VERIFICATIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS CONTINUED FROM NOV-01-2013 TO NOV-08-2013 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE PRO TEM: PETER J. VAN ZANDT; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST AND FREDRICK MORSE AND DENNIS DOYLE'S JOINT MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH ORAL DEPOSITION NOTICE AND REQUEST FOR FEES COSTS AND MONTARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. 1. PLAINTIFF IS COMPELLED TO CONDUCT TWO FOUR HOUR SESSIONS OF QUESTIONING ON THE RECOR. 2. PLAINTIFF SHALL COMPLETE THE FIRST DAY OF DEPOSITION IN GOOD FAITH AND WILL BE HELD ON DECEMBER 10, 2013 AT 10AM AT 601 CALIFORNIA ST. 16TH FLOOR SAN FRANCISCO. 3. ANY AND ALL DEVICES, AIDS INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO STANDS, PILLOWS, GLASSES, BRACES OR OTHER ITEMS PLAINTIFF SHALL REQUIRED TO CONDUCT SUCH DEPOSITION SHALL BE PROVIDED BY PLAINTIFF. 4. PLAINTIFF SHALL COMPLETE THE SECOND DAY OF DEPOSITION IN GOOD FAITH AND WILL BE HELD ON DECEMBER 11, 2013 AT 10AM AT 601 CALIFORNIA ST. 16TH FLOOR SAN FRANCISCO. 5. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY $1,500.00 IN COSTS AND FEES TO THE DEFENDANT FRED MORSE WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS OF THIS COURT ORDER. 6. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY $1,000.00 IN COSTS AND FEES TO DEFENDANT DENNIS DOYLE WITHIN FIFTEEN DAYS OF THIS COURT ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM MARK SCHREIBER, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST AND FREDRICK MORSE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE PROPER SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSE TO THE FOLLOWING PROPOUNDED AND PROOFS OF SERVICE WITHIN TEN DAYS OF THIS COURT ORDER: 1. DEFENDANTS PROPOUNDED SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, NUMBERS 31 THROUGH 68. 2. DEFENDANTS PROPOUNDED FORM INTERROGATORES, SET TWO NUMBERS 4.1 ET SEQ., 4.2 AND 17.1. 3. DEFENDANTS PROPOUNDED REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE NUMBERS 1 THROUGH 60. 4. DEFENDANTS PROPOUNDED REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTIONS OF DOCUMENTS, SET THREE, NUMBER 33 THROUGHT 53. 5. PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE ALL DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DEFENDANT FRED MORSE'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET THREE, NUMBERS 33 THROUGH 53 WITHIN TEN DAYS OF THIS ORDER. 6. IF ANY DOCUMENTS ARE PREVILEGED, PLAINTIFF SHALL PRODUCE A PREVILEGE LOG IDENTIFYING THE CREATOR OF THE DOCUMENT, THE PERSON WHO HAS POSSESSION OF THE ORIGINAL DOCUMENT, A TITLE FOR THE DOCUMENT, THE PREVILEGE CLAIMEDI, WHY THE PRIVILEGE IS APPLICABLE, THE NUMBER OF PAGES OF THE DOCUMENT AND THE DATE THE DOCUMENT WAS CREATED WITHIN TEN DAYS OF THIS ORDER. 7. PLAINTIFF WILL PAY FREDERICK MORSE'S COUNSEL $3,345.00 IN COSTS AND FEES WITH FIFTEEN DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT MORSE FAMILY TRUST, FREDRICK MORSE' MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, VERIFICATIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. 1. PLAINTIFF SHALL SERVE A PROPER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFICATION AND PROOF OF SERVICE TO DEFENDANT'S PROPOUNDED REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTIONS OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, SET TWO, NUMBER 32. 2. PLAINTIFF WILL PRODUCE THE ABOVE VERIFICATION, PROOF OF SERVICE AND FEE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM MARK SCHREIBER, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Things, Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf
Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Plaintiff Leslie K. Black in Pro Per. (415-812-9223)/ Atty. Davin Bacho of Bledsoe, Cathcart, Diestel, Pedersen, Treppa LLP 415-981-5411 appear for defendants Morse Family Trust and Fredrick Morse moving party. / Atty. William E. Joost, Jr. of Law Offices Buresh, Kaplan, Feller & Chang (415-548-7474) appear for defendant Dennis Doyle moving party.

Argued - DEFENDANT MORSE FAMILY TRUST, FREDRICK MORSE' Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And Things, Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff is granted. 1. Plaintiff shall serve a proper code compliant verification and proof of service to defendant's propounded requests for productions of documents and things, set two, number 32. 2. Plaintiff will produce the above verification, proof of service and fee within 15 days of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Mark Schreiber, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Defendants Morse Family Trust And Fredrick Morse'S Motion To Compel Further Disco Responses
Off Calendar Nov-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Plaintiff Leslie K. Black in Pro Per.(415-812-9223) / Atty. Davin Bacho of Bledsoe, Cathcart, Diestel, Pedersen, Treppa LLP 415-981-5411 appear for defendants Morse Family Trust and Fredrick Morse moving party. / Atty. William E. Joost, Jr. of Law Offices Buresh, Kaplan, Feller & Chang (415-548-7474) appear for defendant Dennis Doyle moving party.

Argued - Defendants Morse Family Trust And Fredrick Morse'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses is granted. Plaintiff shall serve proper supplemental response to the following propounded and proofs of service within ten days of this court order: 1. Defendants propounded special interrogatories, set two, numbers 31 through 68. 2. Defendants propounded form interrogatores, set two numbers 4.1 et seq., 4.2 and 17.1. 3. Defendants propounded request for admissions, set one numbers 1 through 60. 4. Defendants propounded requests for productions of documents, set three, number 33 throught 53. 5. Plaintiff shall produce all documents responsive to defendant Fred Morse's request for production of documents, set three, numbers 33 through 53 within ten days of this order. 6. If any documents are previleged, plaintiff shall produce a previlege log identifying the creator of the document, the person who has possession of the original document, a title for the document, the previlege claimedi, why the privilege is applicable, the number of pages of the document and the date the document was created within ten days of this order. 7. Plaintiff will pay Frederick Morse's counsel $3,345.00 in costs and fees with fifteen days of this order. Order signed in open court. Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Davin R. Bacho, Esq. of Bledsoe Cathcart Diestel Pedersen & Treppa LLP, Tel. 415.981.5411, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not opposed. DEFENDANTS MORSE FAMILY TRUST, FREDRICK MORSE'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The Court notes that Defendant violated California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1113(d). The Court could not find any request for a longer memorandum on file. Moreover, Defendant violated California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345 as the Separate Statement did not contain a complete statement of the Requests for Production of Documents and the responses thereto. The Court urges counsel to comply with these Court rules in the future. Nevertheless, the Motion is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to produce further responses to: Form Interrogatories 2.6, 6.7(b), 7.3, 9.1, 12.1 and 50.2-50.6 and Special Interrogatories 6, 19, 22, 23 and 26. Plaintiff is further ordered to produce responsive documents to Requests for Production of Documents 1-31. The Court orders counsel, Mark Hooshmand, to pay sanctions to Defendant of $2500.00. Payment, responses and responsive documents are due within 15 days of this order. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth Riles; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
